title: delphinium bicolor f. montanense
intro: Delphinium bicolor f. montanense, also known as mountain delphinium, is a perennial plant that is native to the western United States. It is a flowering plant with tall spikes of blue and white flowers that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains delphinine, a toxic alkaloid, but in small amounts, it has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and as an expectorant for respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Delphinium bicolor f. montanense include infusions and decoctions, although it is essential to note that the plant should be used with caution due to its toxic properties and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
